type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: No incidents on site

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: The site increased its use of the chemical product that contains Peracetic acid in order to increase the amount of recycled water that the site used in place of fresh water

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The amount of chemical we use that contains Peracetic acid increased from 2013 over 2014.  It was not used in sufficient quantity in 2013 to report to NPRI.
In 2014, however, its use increased significantly due to increased water recycling.  Thus, Peracetic acid use increased and exceeded the reporting threshold, too.
In our original 2014 report, we attributed the Peracetic acid emission to air.  Upon review,  we clarify now, that it is primarily emitted in our effluent to the local wastewater treatment facility.  A negligible amount is also calculated to have been emitted as a VOC or air emission (0.001 metric tonnes).  Since NPRI uses two decimal places, we report now 0.00 metric tonnes releases to air.  The remainder breaks down within, or is released, to water, 11.11 metric tonnes

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Sulfuric acid is used to neutralize a residual sodium hydroxide cleaning ì
solution prior to its discharge into the regional sewage system.  The ì
discharge complies with our sewage use agreement with the Region of ì
Waterloo regarding pH (5.5-9.5).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Sulfuric acid is used to neutralize a residual sodium hydroxide cleaning solution prior to its discharge into the regional sewage system.  The discharge complies with our sewage use agreement with the Region of Waterloo regarding pH (5.5 to 9.5).
